item.title: Keir Starmer and the End of Britain’s Old Certainties
item.description: The decade since Brexit has been one long, painful process of trying — and failing — to make sense of this new reality.

item.title: The Nobel-Winning Psychologist Who Believed He Found the Secret to Happiness
item.description: Why searching for the best is the wrong goal.

item.title: This Approach to Addiction Will Cost Lives
item.description: The Trump administration is going after harm reduction.

item.title: The Silence That Meets the Rape of Palestinians
item.description: Male and female Palestinians describe brutal sexual abuse at the hands of Israel’s prison guards, soldiers, settlers and interrogators.

item.title: Why China Fears the ‘A.I. Girlfriend’
item.description: China is worried A.I. companions could be a drag on productivity. On “Interesting Times,” Kyle Chan, a foreign policy fellow at the Brookings Institution, tells the Opinion columnist Ross Douthat that the government thinks youth “should be engineering the future and building out the start-ups and the future Chinese versions of SpaceX.”

item.title: China Is Worried About A.I. Too
item.description: What if you weren’t worried about A.I. taking your job? That seems to be closer to the reality in China, where keeping pace with the new technology is a much bigger focus than economic disruption. On “Interesting Times,” Kyle Chan, a foreign policy fellow at the Brookings Institution, explains how the countries differ in their anxieties over artificial intelligence.

item.title: China’s Not the Problem. We Are.
item.description: The difference in perspectives between superpowers is shaping the race for A.I. dominance.

item.title: There’s a Pattern of Abuse in America
item.description: M. Gessen and Rachel Louise Snyder on the parallels between authoritarianism and domestic violence.

item.title: Another Trump Appointee Learns It’s Easier to Be a Critic Than a Leader
item.description: We have no F.D.A. director, no permanent C.D.C. director and no surgeon general.

item.title: Thomas Massie Is One of a Vanishing Breed: A Republican Who Will Stand Up to Trump
item.description: An individual with principles and quirks and an aversion to being told what to do.

item.title: Climate Change as a 2026 Campaign Issue
item.description: Readers largely disagree with a guest essay that argued that Democrats should not focus on it. Also: Violence against Latinos; school closings.

item.title: Young Folks Help English Grow Up
item.description: The language is benefiting from a respect for diversity.

item.title: The Trump Dumpster Fire Is Lit
item.description: When MAHA turns into HAHA.

item.title: Britain’s Post-Liberal Disorder
item.description: Britain’s post-liberal disorder.

item.title: He Was Good at Steering the Fed, but He Was a Genius at Ignoring Trump’s Threats
item.description: A model for all who follow.

item.title: China’s Not the Problem. We Are.
item.description: The difference in perspectives between superpowers is shaping the race for A.I. dominance.

item.title: I Don’t Think You Can Even Call This Hypocrisy
item.description: The secrets of one of the architects of the religious right are being revealed. One of the secrets is that they weren’t really secrets.

item.title: Trump’s Iran War Is Punishing America’s Working Class
item.description: Higher fuel prices are a burden on low-income families.

item.title: We Cannot Let Trump Cut Childhood Cancer Research
item.description: More children will die because the Trump administration is cutting pediatric cancer research.

item.title: What You Actually Need to Know About Hantavirus
item.description: What have we learned from Covid?

item.title: Is America Still No. 1?
item.description: The billionaire investor who predicted the 2008 financial crash is bearish on America’s future standing in the world. On “Interesting Times,” Ray Dalio tells the columnist Ross Douthat why.

item.title: Here Are 1.5 Trillion Reasons Not to Trust Pete Hegseth’s Budget
item.description: “You name it, we’re investing in it” does not inspire confidence.

item.title: Trump’s China Policy Has Weakened America
item.description: In Beijing this week, Mr. Trump should not hand China more victories.

item.title: NATO, Please Help. Trump Has No Strategy for Iran.
item.description: NATO should overcome its distaste for President Trump and sends its navies to help open up the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: ‘This Is All a Scam, a Giant Scam’
item.description: Trump is fighting the world’s stupidest culture war.

item.title: Purdue Pharma Is Gone. What Comes Next Could Be Worse.
item.description: The Purdue Pharma case is closed, but the opioid problem remains.

item.title: A New Viral Outbreak. The Same Mistakes All Over Again.
item.description: We should take hantavirus more seriously.

item.title: Democrats Can’t Let This Antisemitic Sex Therapist Win Her Runoff
item.description: Maureen Galindo’s rise demonstrates how even justified rage and despair can curdle into hateful paranoia.

item.title: The Supreme Court Has Left Us in a Dangerous Place
item.description: The ruling in Louisiana v. Callais might drive America’s politics to an even more precarious place of partisan tension and ideological Balkanization.

item.title: The Controversy Over Graham Platner’s Tattoo and Posts
item.description: Readers say David French’s critique of the likely Democratic candidate for the Senate in Maine was too harsh.

item.title: A.I. Claims to Make Our Lives Easier. Does It?
item.description: The A.I. revolution involves a huge transfer of labor — not from worker to machine but from worker to you.

item.title: Unfortunately, We Have to Take Spencer Pratt Seriously as a Politician
item.description: Reality stars and influencers in government are here to stay.

item.title: The Horror of Sexual Assault in Israeli Prisons
item.description: Sexual violence by Israeli forces against Palestinians is widespread, according to new reports and harrowing first-hand accounts. The Opinion columnist Nicholas Kristof details a pattern of systemic abuse and “unrestrained power” within Israeli detention centers that challenges the moral silence of the international community.

item.title: The Shared Feeling of Being Harvested by the Future
item.description: There is a shared sense of precarity that lies beneath the envy and distrust.

item.title: Xi Jinping Is Planning for China’s Final Victory Over the U.S.
item.description: While Trump chases quick wins and flashy optics, Xi plays a generational game for global dominance.

item.title: I Have Some Questions for the Democrats Who Want to Run California
item.description: How will the next governor make housing more affordable? The top five Democratic candidates in California discuss this issue at a forum moderated by Ezra Klein.

item.title: Why Many Americans Choose Not to Have Children
item.description: Readers offer their theories in response to a guest essay.

item.title: China Is Much Weaker Than It Seems. That’s the Problem.
item.description: History shows there is no more productive national asset than freedom.

item.title: The Race to Lower California’s Housing Costs
item.description: Democrats running for California governor join the Opinion columnist Ezra Klein to debate whether manufacturing technology or federal policy can solve the crisis of the state’s skyrocketing home construction costs.

item.title: The Forests of the American West Are in Danger
item.description: The demise of pine forests could make snow virtually disappear from the Southwest in 50 or so years.

item.title: Kevin Warsh Is Already Getting It Wrong
item.description: The Fed’s next leader needs a complete picture of what A.I. will do to the economy.

item.title: Pedro Sánchez Is Donald Trump’s Nemesis, and He’s Winning
item.description: Pedro Sánchez has established an alternative governing philosophy to Trumpism.

item.title: San Jose’s Approach to Homelessness
item.description: San Jose’s mayor, Matt Mahan, tells the Opinion columnist Ezra Klein why California’s reliance on expensive, slow-moving affordable-housing projects has eroded public trust, and argues for a “radically pragmatic” shift toward interim housing and stricter encampment enforcement.

item.title: Katie Porter on the California Forever Project
item.description: How would Katie Porter, a Democratic candidate for California governor, navigate the billionaire-backed California Forever project if elected? She discusses her approach with the Opinion columnist Ezra Klein during a live forum.

item.title: Tom Steyer’s Plan to Fix Modular Housing
item.description: Why has modular housing failed to revolutionize the industry, despite decades of hype? Tom Steyer tells the Opinion columnist Ezra Klein why modular housing start-ups have failed and how California can use its scale to finally make home construction more affordable.

item.title: We’re Thinking About Mental Health Diagnoses All Wrong
item.description: Neither genetics nor brain scans can distinguish a person with depression, A.D.H.D. or autism from one without.

item.title: The Iran War’s Alarming Ripple Effects
item.description: The downstream effects of the Iran war are only beginning. On “The Opinions,” David Wallace-Wells explains how disruptions tied to fertilizer, condoms and other global goods could contribute to famine, disease and rising costs worldwide.

item.title: Facing the Reality of Cellphones in School
item.description: Readers discuss the effects of cellphone bans in school and screen time limits. Also: Close “Alligator Alcatraz”; President Trump’s vulgar language.

item.title: I Ran the N.S.A. This Is How to Defeat China’s Hacker Army.
item.description: Beijing has hacked America, but we have all the tools we need to fight back.
